name: Daikon radish (food medium)
basis: per 100 g fresh weight, raw root

components.name: total protein
components.evidence: Protein 0.60 g/100g; N source when hydrolyzed.

components.name: D-glucose
components.source: USDA FDC 168451 (total sugars 2.5 g) apportioned by glucose:fructose:sucrose ratio in Zaki et al. Front. Plant Sci. 2021 PMC8566945
components.evidence: Glucose is the most abundant root hexose in Raphanus sativus (median 137.65 mg/g DW). Estimate = 54.5% of 2.5 g total sugars.

components.name: D-fructose
components.source: USDA FDC 168451 total sugars apportioned by ratio in Zaki et al. PMC8566945
components.evidence: Fructose second most abundant (median 90.32 mg/g DW); estimate = 35.7% of 2.5 g total sugars.

components.name: sucrose
components.source: USDA FDC 168451 total sugars apportioned by ratio in Zaki et al. PMC8566945; cf. USDA generic radish sucrose 0.10 g
components.evidence: Sucrose is the minor sugar in radish root (median 25.18 mg/g DW, ~10% of sugars). Range 0.1-0.25 g/100g; note current DB listed sucrose as the ONLY sugar, but glucose+fructose dominate.

components.name: malic acid
components.source: Gutierrez & Perez, Sci. Pharm. 2004 (Raphanus sativus chemistry review); Baenas et al. metabolic profiling PMC6273575
components.evidence: One of the principal organic acids of radish root (with oxalic and malonic); TCA-cycle intermediate detected in root metabolome. Fresh-weight amount not quantified in accessible sources; present, likely dominant organic acid.

components.name: malonic acid
components.source: Gutierrez & Perez 2004 review; Frontiers widely-targeted metabolomics fnut.2022.889407
components.evidence: Characteristic and abundant organic acid of radish taproot; not quantified in FW here.

components.name: oxalic acid
components.evidence: Radish root is a low-oxalate vegetable; white radish below detection (~0-2 mg/100g). Root far lower than leaves.

components.name: citric acid
components.source: Baenas et al. PMC6273575; Frontiers fnut.2022.889407
components.evidence: TCA-cycle organic acid detected in radish root metabolome (positively correlated with succinic/fumaric/malic). FW amount not quantified.

components.name: succinic acid
components.evidence: TCA-cycle intermediate detected in radish root; not quantified in FW.

components.name: fumaric acid
components.evidence: TCA-cycle intermediate detected in radish root; not quantified in FW.

components.name: L-phenylalanine
components.evidence: Protein-bound amino acid profile (was missing from current list).

components.name: L-valine
components.evidence: Protein-bound amino acid profile (was missing from current list).

components.name: L-glutamate
components.evidence: Most abundant amino acid; protein-bound profile.

components.name: L-serine
components.evidence: Protein-bound amino acid profile. Replaces the current DB's D-serine (D-enantiomer is an artifact; plant serine is L-serine).

components.name: L-ascorbate (vitamin C)
components.evidence: Vitamin C 22.0 mg/100g; major micronutrient of daikon.

components.name: niacin (B3)
components.evidence: Niacin 0.20 mg/100g (was present as nicotinate but unquantified).

components.name: beta-carotene
components.evidence: Vitamin A / carotene ~0 in the peeled root (carotenoids are in the leaves, not the white root). Kept at ~0; do not treat as a nutrient source here.

components.name: alpha-tocopherol (vitamin E)
components.evidence: Vitamin E reported 0 mg/100g in daikon root.

components.name: iron
components.evidence: Fe 0.40 mg/100g (single consolidated entry; DB had 3 redundant iron species).

components.name: chloride
components.evidence: Present as a genuine root ion; not quantified in USDA panel.

components.name: molybdate (molybdenum)
components.evidence: Genuine trace plant micronutrient; not quantified for daikon. Kept (unlike Ni/Cr/Co which are removed as noise).

components.name: linoleic acid (18:2 n-6)
components.evidence: Linoleic + alpha-linolenic together = PUFA 0.045 g/100g; split ~evenly, linoleic ~0.023 g.

components.name: alpha-linolenic acid (18:3 n-3)
components.evidence: Alpha-linolenic ~0.022 g/100g, remainder of the 0.045 g PUFA.

remove.name: Retinol
remove.reason: Preformed vitamin A (retinol) does not occur in plants; daikon root has only provitamin carotenoids (and those only in trace). FooDB/expansion artifact.

remove.name: Co2+
remove.reason: Cobalt ion; ultra-trace, no measured value in daikon root and no plant requirement at nutritional level. FooDB predicted noise.

remove.name: Nickel
remove.reason: Ultra-trace non-nutritive element, not measured in daikon root. FooDB predicted noise.

remove.name: Chromium
remove.reason: Ultra-trace element, not measured in daikon root. FooDB predicted noise.

remove.name: Iron (Fe3+)
remove.reason: Redundant oxidation-state duplicate of iron; consolidated into single 'iron' entry.

remove.name: Fe2+ mitochondria
remove.reason: Compartment-tagged duplicate of iron (a modeling artifact, not a food nutrient); consolidated into 'iron'.

remove.name: D-Serine
remove.reason: D-enantiomer artifact; plant/protein serine is L-serine. Replaced by L-serine.

remove.name: epsilon-Polylysine
remove.reason: A microbial (Streptomyces albulus) food-preservative additive, not a native daikon metabolite. FooDB noise.

remove.name: (R)-Oxypeucedanin
remove.reason: A furanocoumarin characteristic of Apiaceae/Rutaceae (celery, citrus), biologically implausible in Raphanus (Brassicaceae). FooDB predicted.

remove.name: (±)-erythro-Isoleucine
remove.reason: Stereochemical artifact of isoleucine; the real nutrient (L-isoleucine) is already listed.

missing_added: D-glucose (dominant sugar, absent from current list)
D-fructose (second dominant sugar, absent from current list)
malic acid, malonic acid, oxalic acid, citric acid, succinic acid, fumaric acid (organic acids entirely missing)
quantified amounts for vitamin C, niacin, B-vitamins, minerals, amino acids, fatty acids
L-phenylalanine and L-valine (protein amino acids missing from current list)
corrected sugar picture: current DB listed sucrose as the ONLY sugar, but it is the least abundant
keep_uncovered: 4-methylthio-3-butenyl glucosinolate / glucoraphasatin and its isothiocyanate raphasatin (the defining pungent phytochemicals of daikon; no BiGG met)
sources: USDA FoodData Central FDC 168451 (Radishes, oriental, raw; SR Legacy 11429) - full proximate, sugar total, vitamin, mineral, amino-acid and fatty-acid panel
Zaki et al., Front. Plant Sci. 2021, PMC8566945 - glucose:fructose:sucrose ratios in R. sativus roots (glucose > fructose >> sucrose)
Baenas et al., metabolic profiling of Raphanus sativus cultivars, PMC6273575 - TCA organic acids (malic, citric, succinic, fumaric) in root
Frontiers Nutrition 2022, fnut.2022.889407 - widely-targeted metabolomics of radish taproot organic acids (malonic, citric, oxalic dominant)
Gutierrez & Perez, Raphanus sativus chemistry review - oxalic, malic, malonic as principal root organic acids
notes: Daikon root is a dilute (94.6% water), low-fat medium whose dominant carbon/energy source is free hexose sugars (glucose > fructose, total sugars 2.5 g/100g) plus organic acids (malic/malonic dominant, with oxalic low and citric/succinic/fumaric as TCA intermediates - all previously absent from the DB). The largest correction was the sugar profile: the current record listed only sucrose, which is actually the minor sugar. Amino-N is modest (0.6 g protein, glutamate-rich). Organic-acid fresh-weight amounts are not cleanly quantified in the literature so they are included with null amounts and sourced notes rather than fabricated numbers; sugar split is USDA total apportioned by the published root hexose ratio (flagged as estimate).
